123174.explanation: The executive board, like the municipal council, should be a good reflection of the population. Therefore, proportional representation should be sought when composing the board. This concerns more than just gender and depends heavily on the availability of suitable candidates.

123177.explanation: We pursue a generous terrace policy and support hospitality with flexible permits and attention to sustainability. We encourage evening and night culture and create space for small venues, neighborhood cafes, and cultural meeting places.

123178.explanation: Schiedam has a legal task under the dispersion law and a moral responsibility with all municipalities in the country to ensure good, humane, and safe accommodation for refugees, asylum seekers, and status holders.

123180.explanation: Schiedam shares responsibility with all municipalities in the country to ensure good, humane, and safe accommodation for refugees, asylum seekers, and status holders. If an asylum application is rejected, the applicant must return to their country of origin as soon as possible.

123184.explanation: With the fireworks ban, there is a need for another way to collectively welcome the new year. A fireworks show is a good alternative to private fireworks, but it would be better to find an environmentally and animal-friendly alternative, such as a laser or drone show.

123186.explanation: Parties and festivals too often cause nuisance. Not because the rules are not strict enough, but because norms are exceeded. This cannot be solved by stricter requirements, but by better enforcement of existing ones.

123187.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. But many important places are still not properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such modifications. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in where necessary.

123188.explanation: Local media are important to know what is happening in the local area. The municipality should contribute if the legal contribution is insufficient and certain quality criteria are met. Schiedam and Vlaardingen already cooperate and provide an equal contribution in consultation.

123189.explanation: Too many schools have outdated or temporary buildings. Some relatively new buildings lack good climate control systems. A good building with sufficient fresh air is crucial for quality education.

123190.explanation: If sports clubs get into trouble through no fault of their own, the municipality should step in. This can be done by reviewing the rent paid to the municipality and assisting with sustainability. Through insulation and self-generation, energy bills can be reduced. The municipality helps make those investments.

123193.explanation: A camera gives a false sense of security. Better to have more police on the street than behind a monitor. Cameras can, however, be effectively used to tackle traffic offenders.

123195.explanation: Tensions can often be quickly calmed with a few well-chosen words. The presence of weapons tends to escalate emotions quickly. It is good that de-escalation training is part of the public order officers’ education. That is better than training them to properly use a baton.

123200.explanation: People do not choose social assistance benefits because they do not want to work. Moreover, we must be careful to avoid displacement so that even more people become dependent on benefits.

123201.explanation: The opposite is true: more people should qualify for exemption from municipal taxes. Too often people end up in debt or even homeless because they still have to pay taxes.

123204.explanation: Experiences with pilots show that more income security and less supervision create calm and more social participation, without negative effects on work. With a Schiedam pilot, we get local facts instead of assumptions and can make policy based on trust.

123205.explanation: More and more gasoline, diesel, and LPG cars are being replaced by electric cars. In Schiedam, not everyone can charge on their own property, so there must be sufficient public charging points. This must not come at the expense of disabled parking spaces.

123207.explanation: The municipality must continue to (co)fund the ring bus throughout the city. All neighborhoods must also be accessible in the evenings and weekends with frequent public transport.

123208.explanation: The decision to introduce an environmental zone was made five years ago. It is time to implement that decision in practice now. It directly results in cleaner air and encourages entrepreneurs to invest in sustainable transport.

123209.explanation: Parking problems in old neighborhoods like West and the Gorzen are increasingly getting out of hand. Parking issues are also growing in newer neighborhoods in the northern part of the city. A smart combination of measures is needed, including affordable parking permits.

123210.explanation: The municipality does not decide which care providers offer services in Schiedam, but it can make it easier for care providers to establish themselves. It is difficult to be selective given the shortage of, among others, general practitioners. It is ideal if everyone can find a care provider that suits them.

123211.explanation: Youth care organizations must provide the care that is needed. That involves a certain price. The municipality must first be convinced of the quality before looking at the price. So: the care that is necessary, at the desired quality level, at the lowest possible price. In that order.

123212.explanation: No more tax should be collected than necessary. The municipality should never make a profit from taxes. Taxes should only be raised if there is no other solution, and should be lowered when possible.

123214.explanation: No more tax should be collected than necessary. The municipality should never make a profit from taxes. Taxes should only be raised if there is no other solution, and should be lowered when possible.

123215.explanation: Schiedam has more industrial land than needed. Efforts are underway to upgrade existing industrial sites. Wherever industrial areas become available, residential construction will be promoted. Schieveste and the glass factory site are examples of this.

123219.explanation: There is no agricultural land in Schiedam. Where meadow areas are unsuitable for solar panels, for example for cultural-historical reasons, it is already prohibited.

123220.explanation: The theater must contribute to the municipality's budget cuts, but that should not come at the expense of funds saved for replacements. When items wear out or break, they must be able to be replaced.

123221.explanation: As far as it fits within the guidelines of the coffeeshop policy, the municipality allows new coffeeshops. The more soft drugs sold through known and regulated channels, the less happens illegally and out of sight.

123222.explanation: Many social rental homes have been demolished in Schiedam in recent years. To ensure that enough social rental housing is available, sufficient construction is needed. A minimum of 30% applies.

123229.explanation: Locations have been designated where temporary housing can be built. It now needs to be ensured that these homes are actually constructed. This will reduce the pressure on the overstressed housing market.

123230.explanation: As far as possible within the law and regional agreements, people with ties to Schiedam have priority for housing in Schiedam.

123174.explanation: The college of mayor and aldermen must consist of good administrators. This may mean that the college has more women than men, or vice versa. For the Schiedam VVD, quality—not gender—is paramount.

123177.explanation: The Schiedam VVD supports fewer rules for entrepreneurs and a lively city with sufficient hospitality offerings. Therefore, we believe that cafes and restaurants should have the freedom to determine their own opening hours.

123178.explanation: The Schiedam VVD does not want to voluntarily accommodate asylum seekers in Schiedam. As soon as national law no longer requires us to house asylum seekers, we want this asylum accommodation to close.

123180.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers who still reside in the Netherlands, illegal immigrants, must leave the country as soon as possible and return to their country of origin.

123184.explanation: The Schiedam VVD supports a fireworks show on New Year's Eve, preferably initiated by private organizations. We would like the municipality to play a facilitating and coordinating role in organizing this fireworks show.

123186.explanation: The Schiedam VVD is not in favor of patronizing rules. We support a lively city with attractive events and therefore do not see the need for stricter noise standards at festivals and parties.

123187.explanation: The Schiedam VVD believes in a municipality accessible to people with disabilities. This allows everyone to participate in society as much as possible and develop themselves. The municipality can financially contribute to making buildings more accessible in this context.

123188.explanation: The Schiedam VVD supports a frugal government focused on core tasks. We see no reason why more Schiedam taxpayers' money should go to the local broadcaster.

123189.explanation: The Schiedam VVD is in favor of building modern and adequate school buildings and believes old school buildings should be improved to meet the requirements for good education so that children can receive quality education in a pleasant environment.

123190.explanation: The Schiedam VVD supports sports clubs because they contribute to health, social cohesion, and youth development. If clubs experience difficulties, temporary municipal support under clear conditions can help preserve sports facilities.

123193.explanation: For years, the Schiedam VVD has said 'camera surveillance puts a face to criminals.' We support expanding camera surveillance to make Schiedam's streets safer and to punish troublemakers more quickly.

123195.explanation: The Schiedam VVD believes it is important that our enforcement officers are equipped with adequate means to perform their work safely. Therefore, we believe that after completing the necessary training, official enforcement officers (BOAs) should be equipped with a baton.

123200.explanation: The Schiedam VVD believes participation is central. Those receiving social assistance benefits are expected to contribute something back to society. Meaningful social work increases self-esteem, work rhythm, and job opportunities, with attention to tailored support and personal circumstances.

123201.explanation: All Schiedam residents use the sewer system and produce waste. It is unacceptable for taxpaying Schiedam residents that some residents do not contribute at all. Therefore, we oppose full exemption from municipal taxes for minimum income households.

123204.explanation: A basic income is an expensive experiment that does not reward working and shifts the costs onto hardworking Schiedam residents. The Schiedam VVD chooses to guide residents towards work with targeted support rather than an unproven trial.

123205.explanation: The Schiedam VVD opts for freedom of choice and accessibility, not for favoring a small group at the expense of other motorists. Scarce parking spaces are distributed fairly and not reserved just for one type of car.

123207.explanation: Accessibility is not a luxury but a basic provision for Schiedam residents, including those who are not mobile. The Schiedam VVD supports smart and efficient tailored transport that connects neighborhoods without wasting millions on empty buses.

123208.explanation: An environmental zone costs a lot of money, limits accessibility, and mostly affects residents and local entrepreneurs. The Schiedam VVD wants Schiedam to focus on smart and feasible measures for cleaner air without unnecessary regulatory pressure.

123209.explanation: Introducing paid parking in more locations means extra rules, higher costs, and less freedom. The Schiedam VVD chooses practical solutions that keep Schiedam accessible and livable.

123210.explanation: The Schiedam VVD chooses quality and freedom of choice within existing care, not based on belief, religion, or culture. Tailoring care to religion or culture harms integration and leads to higher costs.

123211.explanation: The Schiedam VVD aims for a healthy balance: good youth care at a fair price, not unnecessarily expensive contracts. Efficient use of taxpayers' money without losing sight of quality is crucial for us.

123212.explanation: High property tax rates (OZB) burden residents and entrepreneurs and affect Schiedam residents’ finances. The Schiedam VVD strives to lower the OZB by critically reviewing expenditures and managing municipal spending frugally.

123214.explanation: The Schiedam VVD supports a hospitable city without imposing extra charges on tourists. Raising the tourist tax would mainly harm local hospitality businesses and shops.

123215.explanation: A strong local economy is good for Schiedam. Expanding industrial areas means employment, economic strength, and new opportunities for entrepreneurs.

123219.explanation: The Schiedam VVD chooses not to pollute Schiedam's scarce agricultural land with solar panels. Schiedam should focus on solar energy on rooftops so that farmers can continue farming and energy is produced sustainably.

123220.explanation: Schiedam has a fine theater. Renovating it will cost millions. The Schiedam VVD wants to invest wisely and sees safety, a clean city, and housing construction as higher priorities than an expensive theater renovation.

123221.explanation: The Schiedam VVD sees no need for an additional coffeeshop besides the ones already licensed.

123222.explanation: Social rental housing is important, but mandating 30% in new construction can slow housing production. Schiedam has an above-average number of social rental homes. The Schiedam VVD chooses a realistic mix of rental and ownership so residents can build a housing career.

123229.explanation: The Schiedam VVD focuses on flexible solutions that make housing quickly available without prolonged bureaucracy. Temporary housing helps alleviate pressure on the housing market and gives Schiedam residents a roof over their heads faster.

123230.explanation: The housing shortage mainly affects Schiedam residents who work here, have school-going children, or have been on the waiting list for years. The Schiedam VVD believes that Schiedam residents should be given as much priority as possible in the allocation of rental homes.

123174.explanation: We believe it is important that aldermen are appointed based on expertise, competencies, and suitability for the position. A mandatory male-female ratio should never come at the expense of quality or professional competence. However, we do think that the council should be a reflection of society.

123177.explanation: We believe that entrepreneurs should have the freedom to determine their own opening hours as long as this does not cause nuisance to neighbors. This way, we promote entrepreneurship and a lively city life without losing sight of livability.

123178.explanation: We believe that Schiedam must remain a safe place for refugees needing protection. Closing asylum centers would seriously undermine our moral duty and shows that we do not take our responsibility, solidarity, and humanity seriously.

123180.explanation: We believe that rejected asylum seekers should also be treated humanely. Providing shelter is a demonstration of our moral duty and shows that we seriously take responsibility, solidarity, and compassion.

123184.explanation: We believe that a municipal fireworks display can be a safe and festive alternative. At the same time, a fireworks ban in Schiedam must be strictly enforced so that nuisance, danger to animals, and pollution are effectively limited.

123186.explanation: We believe that festivals and parties are important for the cultural life and livability of the city. Stricter noise standards should not unnecessarily restrict events. We advocate a balanced approach where nuisance is tackled where necessary without hindering culture and enjoyment.

123187.explanation: We believe that everyone, regardless of disability, should have access to shops, hospitality, and facilities. Therefore, we support the municipality financially assisting entrepreneurs to make their buildings more accessible, promoting inclusivity and equal opportunities in Schiedam.

123188.explanation: We believe that local broadcasters should primarily generate their own income through advertising, subscriptions, or other means. Public funds must always be spent responsibly.

123189.explanation: We believe that good education begins with a safe and healthy learning environment. Therefore, the municipality must invest in improving all school buildings so that students and teachers in Schiedam can learn and work optimally, with quality and safety always a priority.

123190.explanation: We believe that sports clubs are important for society. The municipality can provide customized financial support, provided this ultimately contributes to healthy finances and sustainable operation of the club, ensuring support is effective and responsible.

123193.explanation: We believe safety is important, but extra camera surveillance is not an effective solution and should not unnecessarily limit residents' privacy. We advocate other ways to increase safety, such as good enforcement, prevention, and cooperation with the community.

123195.explanation: We believe that public order officers play an important role in enforcement and safety, but equipping them with batons increases the risk of violence and escalation. We advocate well-trained officers who can ensure safety through communication and prevention.

123200.explanation: We believe that people on welfare have the right to support without mandatory work. Participation and meaningful work can be better encouraged through voluntary action and guidance, so everyone has opportunities without pressure, coercion, or stigmatization.

123201.explanation: We believe that residents with low incomes are entitled to financial support where necessary. Waiving municipal taxes helps vulnerable groups make ends meet and prevents poverty, thereby preserving social justice and inclusivity in Schiedam.

123204.explanation: We believe financial security is important, but a basic income is drastic and complex to implement properly. We prefer targeted social support and poverty reduction that is effective, affordable, and responsible, so vulnerable residents are optimally helped.

123205.explanation: We believe it is important to facilitate electric driving, but the number of charging points must correspond to demand. Too many or too few spots leads to inefficiency. Therefore, we advocate a balanced approach that carefully aligns supply and demand.

123207.explanation: We believe good and accessible transport is essential for residents, especially for the elderly, youth, and people without their own transport. Therefore, we support investment in tailored transport so that mobility, social connection, and participation in society in all neighborhoods are maintained.

123208.explanation: We believe clean air and a healthy living environment are important, but an environmental zone can cause unnecessary restrictions and costs for residents and entrepreneurs.

123209.explanation: We believe parking must be well regulated, but the introduction of parking permits should not be without consultation. Therefore, we advocate that neighborhood consultations always precede this to ensure residents are heard and solutions for parking nuisance are carefully and fairly coordinated.

123210.explanation: We believe care must be personal and respectful. Residents should always have access to culturally sensitive care, where the caregiver considers faith, culture, and belief, ensuring care is tailored with understanding and trust.

123211.explanation: We believe the quality of youth care is more important than the lowest price. When selecting organizations, the municipality must consider expertise, customization, and the effectiveness of assistance so that children and families in Schiedam receive the care they truly need.

123212.explanation: We believe municipal taxes must be fair and responsible. Lowering property tax decreases income for municipal tasks and must be weighed carefully. We advocate a balanced approach in which services and tax burdens remain in balance.

123214.explanation: We believe tourist tax is a means to cover tourism costs, but any increase must be carefully considered. Excessive tax can deter visitors, while too low a tax limits income for maintenance and facilities. We advocate a balanced approach.

123215.explanation: We believe economic development is important, but expanding business parks must be carefully weighed against living environment, traffic, and environment. We advocate an approach that provides room for entrepreneurship without harming residential and living quality.

123219.explanation: We believe sustainable energy is important, but installation of solar panels must fit the environment. We advocate an approach focused on roofs and business parks so that the energy transition is efficient and responsible.

123220.explanation: We believe culture is important, but a large-scale renovation of Theater aan de Schie entails too high costs. We advocate maintaining and improving existing facilities in a financially responsible manner.

123221.explanation: We believe new coffee shops are undesirable. Their establishment can lead to nuisance and security issues. We advocate maintaining the current situation and a policy that protects the living environment and public order in Schiedam without expanding coffee shops.

123222.explanation: We believe a sufficient supply of social rental housing is essential for affordable housing. By making at least 30% of new homes social, we ensure a balanced housing market and better housing opportunities for residents with lower incomes in Schiedam.

123229.explanation: We believe temporary housing is important to relieve pressure on the housing market. However, this can only be done in suitable locations. This way residents can find housing faster, waiting times are shortened, and livability in Schiedam is maintained.

123230.explanation: We believe Schiedam residents should be prioritized when renting homes. This strengthens ties with the city and ensures local residents find suitable housing faster while maintaining attention to a fair and transparent allocation system.

123174.explanation: Although we strive for a balanced composition that reflects society well, we do not believe this should be a mandatory requirement.

123177.explanation: D66 Schiedam wants to give more space to hospitality in our city. To create a vibrant and lively city, we want to offer hospitality entrepreneurs more freedom.

123178.explanation: D66 Schiedam believes we must fulfill our legal duty. We provide shelter to people fleeing war and violence, but we also think it is important that all municipalities contribute fairly.

123180.explanation: D66 Schiedam wants no one to have to sleep on the street, including people not entitled to stay here. However, we must work quickly on their return.

123184.explanation: D66 Schiedam is in favor of an alternative to the ban on consumer fireworks. This alternative does not always have to take the form of a fireworks show.

123186.explanation: D66 Schiedam wants to create space for a vibrant and lively city. We can maintain current rules, taking possible nuisance into account.

123187.explanation: D66 Schiedam supports a city where everyone can participate. We believe entrepreneurs themselves bear responsibility and the municipality does not have to spend money on this. However, the municipality can ensure better accessibility in other ways.

123188.explanation: D66 Schiedam considers a strong local broadcaster very important because it contributes to local democracy. Starting in 2028, local broadcasters will merge into regional broadcasters funded by the national government.

123189.explanation: D66 Schiedam believes the municipality must ensure a good environment for children to attend school. Quality education is important for equal opportunities, and a pleasant school building is the foundation for that.

123190.explanation: D66 Schiedam sees sports clubs as places where residents work on their health and meet each other. The municipality supports clubs by arranging good facilities, but the club itself remains responsible for its finances.

123193.explanation: Crime in Schiedam has decreased in recent years. However, we see that more and more residents feel unsafe. We do not think more cameras are the solution. Instead, together with residents and partners, we focus on the places that are perceived as unsafe.

123195.explanation: D66 does not consider arming enforcement officers (boa’s) a good idea: weapons do not solve problems. Furthermore, arming boa’s causes confusion about the roles of the police and enforcement. D66 Schiedam sees no reason for additional measures.

123200.explanation: D66 Schiedam believes it is important that people do what they can. This means that people must receive the right support to help them on their way. For some, that's a job; for others, a form of social work or day activities. This must always be tailor-made.

123201.explanation: D66 Schiedam sees debt forgiveness as part of the solution for people struggling to make ends meet. Additionally, we find it important that everyone who needs help receives it quickly so they can eventually pay for themselves.

123204.explanation: D66 Schiedam does not see a basic income as a solution. Multiple studies have shown that experiments with basic income have not been successful and do not lead to the desired results.

123205.explanation: D66 Schiedam wants us to find other ways to travel. We want more space for pedestrians, cyclists, shared cars, and public transport. Furthermore, we encourage clean modes of transport such as electric cars.

123207.explanation: D66 Schiedam wants us to find other ways to travel. Therefore, we continue to advocate regionally for better public transport and good connections between our neighborhoods. We seek smart transport solutions.

123208.explanation: D66 Schiedam is committed to a healthy and livable city. D66 in Schiedam wants our city to be healthy and pleasant to live in. Therefore, we want areas where polluting cars are not allowed. We encourage clean modes of transport and look for smart solutions for transport and logistics.

123209.explanation: D66 Schiedam wants us to find other ways to travel. We want more space for pedestrians, cyclists, shared cars, and public transport and less space for parked cars. Additionally, we want smarter alternatives such as parking hubs at the edges of neighborhoods.

123210.explanation: We do not believe that facilitating separate care contributes to an inclusive society. However, D66 Schiedam believes the care someone receives must be inclusive, regardless of a person’s background, religion, age, orientation, or gender.

123211.explanation: D66 Schiedam believes young people have the right to a safe, healthy, and promising future. Here, we consider a good balance between price and quality important.

123212.explanation: To perform the municipality’s tasks well, we must use limited financial resources wisely. D66 Schiedam wants expenditures to be critically reviewed and to prevent increases in property tax and other levies. Unfortunately, lowering them is not feasible at this time.

123214.explanation: If we want to be an attractive city, we need money for that. Because the government provides less and less funding, we must secure income ourselves. This is one of the few ways the municipality can generate its own revenue.

123215.explanation: Space in Schiedam is limited. D66 Schiedam prefers building homes. Moreover, we see more opportunities in transforming existing industrial areas rather than expanding.

123219.explanation: D66 Schiedam believes everyone should be free to make their own choices. Additionally, D66 Schiedam values sustainability highly and encourages everyone to make sustainable choices.

123220.explanation: D66 Schiedam is pleased with the theater. At this moment, renewal is financially not feasible. Furthermore, the cultural sector in Schiedam is under pressure; therefore, D66 Schiedam wants attention for the entire sector.

123221.explanation: Under current policy, there is room for four coffeeshops in Schiedam. We see no reason to increase this number. If there is a reason in the future, D66 Schiedam is open to reconsidering the number of coffeeshops.

123222.explanation: D66 Schiedam wants everyone who wants to stay in Schiedam to be able to do so. Currently, Schiedam has far more social rental homes than the national average. D66 Schiedam therefore wants more balance in the housing supply and does not want 30% social rental housing built everywhere by obligation.

123229.explanation: The housing shortage in Schiedam is high; therefore, we want to build new homes faster. To address the housing shortage quickly, temporary homes can be a provisional solution.

123230.explanation: The housing shortage in Schiedam is high and there are too few homes. We do not see granting priority as a solution to this shortage. D66 Schiedam wants everyone who wishes to stay in Schiedam to be able to do so. Therefore, we want to build more but also look for other smart solutions.

123174.explanation: It is about the best candidate.

123186.explanation: Everyone has the right to a good night's rest.

123188.explanation: Local news is important for all Schiedam residents.

123189.explanation: Young people are the future. For NOW FOR Later.

123201.explanation: People with only state pension (AOW) should be spared.

123204.explanation: Schiedam does not need to be a frontrunner.

123205.explanation: That comes at the expense of parking spaces for ordinary Schiedam residents.

123208.explanation: Schiedam must remain accessible for plumbers.

123209.explanation: Let the residents decide themselves through a referendum.

123210.explanation: As far as we are concerned, no Turkish nursing home.

123214.explanation: Is necessary to keep the city center livable

123215.explanation: Comes at the expense of Beatrixpark

123221.explanation: We already have four. Bad for the youth

123230.explanation: We strive to push the boundaries of the law

123174.explanation: The coalition parties nominate candidates based on quality and suitability. Diversity in the municipal executive is valuable and something to aspire to, but quality and administrative strength remain leading.

123177.explanation: The CDA believes it is good to apply a clear closing time so that residents of the city center can enjoy peace after a night out. Furthermore, unfair competition between hospitality businesses is prevented.

123178.explanation: CDA Schiedam takes responsibility regarding asylum seekers challenges. The national government decides what we as a municipality must accommodate and we adhere to that. If this obligation changes or ceases, Schiedam's role in asylum reception will be reassessed.

123180.explanation: The CDA is clear on this: asylum seekers who have been rejected after a thorough procedure have no right to stay and must leave the Netherlands and return to their country of origin.

123184.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality should look at what is possible if there is a need for something communal for neighborhood residents. A fireworks show organized by the municipality of Schiedam is not necessary.

123186.explanation: There are applicable standards and no reason to deviate from these. Enforcement is important in this respect.

123187.explanation: Accessible buildings for everyone are important. As far as the CDA is concerned, the municipality should encourage entrepreneurs with subsidies to make their buildings accessible for people with disabilities.

123188.explanation: The local broadcaster is important for delivering independent local news. The CDA expects the local broadcaster to focus on TV, radio, and social media, as they currently do. The CDA is cautious about additional financial expenditures; extra money is not needed.

123189.explanation: The CDA considers good school buildings extremely important for our children in Schiedam. Where improvements are needed, they must be made. Too much backlog has accumulated over the past decades.

123190.explanation: Sports clubs are kept running by volunteers. The CDA wants to support (sports) clubs having difficulties, if necessary financially. It must always be examined how a (sports) club can sustain itself again in the long term.

123193.explanation: In places where necessary, the CDA believes there should be camera surveillance as a preventive measure and to identify perpetrators of criminal behavior.

123195.explanation: The CDA supports expanding the powers and protection tools for enforcement officers (boa’s), including batons, to better protect them during their work. However, good training and clear regulations are important.

123200.explanation: The CDA believes it is reasonable to expect a person receiving welfare benefits to engage in socially valuable work. Of course, this must always be considered based on the individual's capabilities.

123201.explanation: Municipal charges are high and not every Schiedam resident can easily afford them. The CDA advocates for municipal charges to be as low as possible. For residents who truly cannot bear these charges, remission should remain possible under clear conditions and with customization.

123204.explanation: The CDA finds a basic income neither solidarity-based, just, nor affordable. The participation income is a better idea. Instead of giving everyone 'free money', the participation income rewards people who actively participate in society.

123205.explanation: In the coming decades, more and more cars will be electric. It is important that there are enough charging stations for this. Charging stations should not displace existing parking spaces. There must be space for all cars: conventional, hybrid, and fully electric.

123207.explanation: Public transport within Schiedam must really improve. A neighborhood like Schiedam-Noord is often not reachable by public transport at all. The CDA wants every neighborhood to be accessible by public transport during the day and evening. The funding for this should come from MRDH, which is responsible for transport in our region.

123208.explanation: The introduction of an environmental zone hits entrepreneurs hard. The CDA therefore chooses a gradual transition. New vehicles must comply with strict standards, reducing the number of polluting vehicles over time.

123209.explanation: The CDA believes residents should be able to give their opinion before an area is designated as a permit parking zone. Permit parking will only be introduced with sufficient support. After a survey, the municipality will not conduct a similar survey again within a few years.

123210.explanation: If it is possible to choose a care provider, that is fine. However, this is not always possible and many do not find it necessary. Therefore, it is not a matter of always being able to choose, but depends on the patient's wish, availability, and the care that needs to be provided.

123211.explanation: The CDA thinks that alongside price, the quality to be delivered is equally important. We find it inappropriate that low-price contractors are contracted. The ultimate result, good youth care, is paramount.

123212.explanation: The CDA believes municipal taxes should be as low as possible for everyone. The tax increases of recent years should, where possible, be reversed.

123214.explanation: Tourist tax in Schiedam is much lower than, for example, Rotterdam. The CDA believes this should be more balanced by increasing the Schiedam rate.

123215.explanation: There is a great need for housing in Schiedam, but also for good business parks. The CDA believes these developments should proceed in parallel to maintain employment in the long term.

123219.explanation: The CDA does not want to prohibit solar panels on agricultural land, but they must fit within the area. Rules and standards are important here.

123220.explanation: The CDA believes that the Theater aan de Schie currently functions adequately. Investment is allowed where it really cannot be otherwise, but large-scale renewal at the expense of Schiedam residents is, in the CDA's view, especially in times of budget cuts, not an option.

123221.explanation: The CDA has always had great difficulties with the number of coffeeshops in our city. The trade in drugs must be combated and therefore we support a phasing-out policy for coffeeshops.

123222.explanation: The CDA believes that both social rental housing and homes in higher price classes must be built. A good principle is that 30% is built in the social rental and/or purchase category.

123229.explanation: The demand for housing is so great that the CDA believes all possibilities to add housing must be seized, whether temporary homes, new construction, or conversion of office buildings.

123230.explanation: The CDA believes that Schiedam residents should primarily have priority in the allocation of rental homes. This is important for preserving communities, social networks, and the connection with the city of Schiedam.

123174.explanation: It is not about being male or female but about the capacity.

123177.explanation: No, that is not fair regarding the size of the companies. Larger companies have more staff, which makes it easier than for a small entrepreneur. That is not fair competition.

123178.explanation: Schiedam has enough challenges, and financially the situation is not rosy either. First get the basics in order, then look further.

123180.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers are illegal and must leave the Netherlands as soon as possible before they become undocumented. They then have nothing to lose, which can cause major problems.

123184.explanation: Certainly. It is a festive tradition to close out the year where fireworks belong.

123186.explanation: That entirely depends on the situation. Much has to do with the location where the party or festival takes place.

123187.explanation: That is not a task of the municipality.

123188.explanation: The local broadcaster receives a subsidy, and the municipality does not need to provide additional financial support.

123189.explanation: The question applies to ALL school buildings. It is a trade-off that must be made by looking at the municipality's financial situation.

123190.explanation: That depends entirely on the situation.

123201.explanation: But not below social assistance level.

123205.explanation: Only when a neighborhood consultation on this has taken place.

123207.explanation: The municipality already provides for that.

123209.explanation: Parking permits throughout Schiedam or none at all.

123220.explanation: That is not a task of the municipality.

123221.explanation: That should be possible as long as the maximum total of 4 coffeeshops allowed in Schiedam is not exceeded.

123174.explanation: Select based on quality, regardless of gender and/or background. We do not do quotas.

123177.explanation: As long as it is within the legal framework and no persistent nuisance or incidents occur.

123180.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers must return to their country of origin as soon as possible.

123184.explanation: We want to wait and see the current situation regarding the national ban and the alternatives that arise.

123189.explanation: Improve school buildings where necessary, renovate where possible.

123190.explanation: In unforeseen circumstances, the municipality can provide support provided the club has a legitimate existence.

123193.explanation: In places with many incidents, it must be possible to place temporary camera units.

123174.explanation: Alles voor Schiedam believes the council does not necessarily have to consist of an equal number of men and women because expertise, experience, and suitability are more important than strict gender distribution.

123177.explanation: If cafes and restaurants are allowed to stay open all night unlimited, this can cause nuisance for residents. Municipal rules on closing times ensure clarity, predictability, and a balance between entrepreneurs' freedom and residents' right to peace and safety.

123178.explanation: Alles voor Schiedam wants the asylum reception to close as soon as possible, if and when laws and regulations allow, because it causes unrest, nuisance, a reduced sense of security, and pressure on facilities.

123180.explanation: Asylum seekers who have completed all procedures and whose asylum applications have been rejected must return to their country of origin as soon as possible.

123184.explanation: Due to the national fireworks ban, the tradition changes, but New Year's Eve remains a community celebration. The municipality facilitates and subsidizes where necessary so existing organizations can organize events, instead of doing everything themselves.

123186.explanation: Festivals and parties have contributed to the positive image of Schiedam in recent years. However, the municipality and organizers must work well together and make agreements on sound levels, end times, communication with residents, and enforcement so that events remain enjoyable and nuisance is limited.

123187.explanation: Accessibility is a social responsibility that comes with entrepreneurship and equal treatment. Public funds should be used for general provisions, not to subsidize individual business investments.

123188.explanation: In the current times of budget cuts, "extra" money for local broadcasters, according to Alles voor Schiedam, is not a good idea.

123189.explanation: There is a carefully prepared plan to improve school buildings. There is no need for all school buildings to be renewed or improved during the upcoming council period.

123190.explanation: Sports and community life are crucial for health and cohesion. The municipality can support associations with external problems, but not with mismanagement or poor governance. Community funds are not intended to fix internal mismanagement.

123193.explanation: More camera surveillance can contribute to increasing safety and the feeling of safety in Schiedam. When used targeted and carefully, it helps prevent and solve incidents and supports the efforts of police and enforcement.

123195.explanation: Bailiffs (BOA’s) monitoring safety increasingly face aggression and threats. A baton can contribute to their own safety and authoritative performance of their work, provided it is accompanied by clear rules, good training, and appropriate use.

123200.explanation: We want people receiving social assistance benefits to remain socially active and contribute to society. We want to include this in the social assistance rules. This is also intended to increase the chances of employment.

123201.explanation: Debt remission prevents people with low incomes from getting into financial problems or debt. Without this scheme, people may be unable to pay their rent, energy, or food.

123204.explanation: A basic income trial can be expensive and tie up municipal resources. Schiedam has more urgent social issues such as poverty reduction, youth work, or housing problems that need attention first.

123205.explanation: The municipality should only designate additional charging points if there is sufficient demand. Charging facilities must match actual use, so that space in the city is used efficiently and fairly.

123207.explanation: Schiedam needs a reliable and accessible public transport connection between all parts of the city. Tailored transport should complement this basic service, not replace it.

123208.explanation: An environmental zone in Schiedam limits entrepreneurial freedom, makes deliveries and accessibility difficult, burdens residents with older cars, and has only a limited effect on air quality.

123209.explanation: Parking pressure is too high in several neighborhoods of our city. Regulated parking can help reduce parking nuisance and provide more control over livability and safety. At the same time, we want to keep costs for permit holders as low as possible.

123210.explanation: Good and accessible care is paramount and, in our view, should not be tied to religion, culture, or belief.

123211.explanation: For Alles voor Schiedam, quality comes first, not price.

123212.explanation: We must maintain property tax (OZB) levels. The OZB should not be used as the balancing item in the municipal budget.

123214.explanation: Tourist tax provides extra income for maintenance of public spaces, tourist facilities, and the many monuments in Schiedam. This way, visitors share in the costs and preservation of the historical heritage, keeping the city attractive.

123215.explanation: Physical expansion of industrial areas is not necessary and not desirable. Beautiful developments are already planned within existing space, such as MICS in the ’s-Gravelandsepolder. We want to encourage these developments and make Schiedam more attractive by combining work and living well.

123219.explanation: Unless agriculture can be combined with energy production (for example agrivoltaics), allowing farmers additional income without complete loss of agricultural production.

123220.explanation: Renewing the Theater is primarily not a municipal responsibility.

123221.explanation: We already have more than enough coffee shops. New establishments can increase social problems or attract crime.

123222.explanation: Schiedam needs a diverse housing mix, not a fixed percentage for each new development location.

123229.explanation: Alles voor Schiedam acknowledges the benefits of flexible housing but believes each project must carefully consider where these homes are located and for whom, to meet the needs of Schiedam residents.

123230.explanation: By giving priority to residents of Schiedam for allocating rental homes, we increase opportunities for people connected to the city.

123174.explanation: Equality is central. The executive board must be a good reflection of all Schiedam residents. This way everyone recognizes themselves in the city government, decisions are better and fairer, and every vote counts.

123177.explanation: Cafés and restaurants should, in principle, be able to set their own opening hours. It remains important to consider peace, health, and livability for neighbors. Suitable agreements can be made through consultation with residents.

123178.explanation: Humanity and solidarity come first. Problems are solved by taking responsibility together, not by excluding people. Therefore, asylum seekers should be able to participate in society as soon as possible.

123180.explanation: Even without residency status, people remain human beings. Schiedam must prevent people from becoming homeless and ensure humane shelter and access to basic care. Everyone has the right to dignified treatment and the protection of human rights.

123184.explanation: Consumer fireworks cause fear, stress, injuries, and nuisance. People, animals, and the environment deserve protection. Therefore, a fireworks-free alternative, such as a light or drone show, is preferred. A professional fireworks show is only acceptable if no one suffers harm.

123186.explanation: Residents and animals can suffer greatly from light and noise at events. There is room for events, but only in places where neighbors experience as little nuisance as possible and where they do not cause damage to nature and animal habitats.

123187.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate independently. By helping entrepreneurs make adaptations, shops and hospitality businesses become accessible to people with disabilities. Public and communal spaces must always be accessible as well.

123188.explanation: Local media provide reliable information and connection in the city. A strong and independent local broadcaster is important for a well-functioning democracy.

123189.explanation: Good school buildings provide a healthy learning environment. Schools and schoolyards should be made more sustainable and greener, for example with natural playgrounds and school gardens. Children can thus learn, play, and grow better.

123190.explanation: Sports connect people and keep Schiedam residents healthy. Sports clubs must be able to continue to exist, so sports remain accessible to everyone. Every neighborhood should have space for sports facilities for young and old, such as exercise gardens, sports fields, and skate parks.

123193.explanation: Camera surveillance infringes on privacy and does not address problems at their root. It often relocates nuisance to another place. Safety mainly arises from prevention, trust, and strong, social neighborhoods.

123195.explanation: Safety arises through de-escalation and prevention. More weapons often cause extra tension and do not make the city safer. Problems are better solved through calm, dialogue, and early intervention.

123200.explanation: People should be supported based on trust. Mandatory counter-obligations cause stress and uncertainty and do not solve poverty. Residents and their needs should be central, with a broad, humane approach to social assistance.

123201.explanation: To prevent people from falling into or remaining in poverty, municipal taxes should be written off or assumed earlier. This helps people make ends meet and provides more financial peace.

123204.explanation: A basic income contributes to more equality and financial security. A pilot can examine whether this helps reduce poverty and gives people greater financial peace.

123205.explanation: Clean transport is necessary for clean air and a healthy city. Schiedam must have sufficient green-energy charging points for electric cars, integrated into the streetscape. No additional parking spaces will be added, preserving space for greenery, walking, cycling, and playing.

123207.explanation: Transport must be safe, accessible, and sustainable for everyone. Tailored transport helps, among others, the elderly and less mobile people. This way everyone can move around and participate in society.

123208.explanation: Clean transport is necessary for clean air and a healthy city. Environmental zones exclude polluting traffic and thus improve air quality. That leads to less emissions and a pleasant and healthier living environment for everyone.

123209.explanation: Parking permits help limit the number of cars in Schiedam. This creates more space for green areas and improves safety and livability in neighborhoods. Paid parking applies throughout the municipality.

123210.explanation: Care must fit who someone is. Free choice builds more trust because people feel safe and understood. Care and assistance must be of good quality and consider someone’s background, identity, and lifestyle.

123211.explanation: Youth care must focus on quality and appropriate help. Children, young people, and young adults should get what they need, not what is cheapest. Early prevention is important, with good youth work playing a major role.

123212.explanation: Taxes on houses and buildings do not need to be lowered but can be smarter. A green discount makes sustainability more attractive. Owners pay less for energy-efficient homes. Tax revenues are necessary to keep the city fair and livable.

123214.explanation: Tourism creates jobs and supports art, culture, and heritage, but also puts extra (environmental) pressure on the city. A higher tourist tax can be used to improve facilities, public spaces, and the environment, and to make tourism more sustainable.

123215.explanation: Expansion of industrial sites is not desirable. Sites without a future or with many nuisances can better be transformed into places for housing or green spaces. Where companies remain, space must be used more intelligently. Large distribution centers and data centers do not fit in there.

123219.explanation: Energy must be generated sustainably with respect for people, animals, and nature. Solar panels belong on roofs, facades, parking lots, and sound barriers. Agricultural land is meant for food or can be returned to nature. Therefore, solar panels on agricultural land are undesirable.

123220.explanation: Theater and culture bring people together. Renovation of Theater aan de Schie is necessary to make the building accessible, including for people with disabilities, and to make it more sustainable. This way, culture remains accessible to all Schiedam residents.

123221.explanation: When establishing a new coffeeshop, it is important to consider if and how it fits within a neighborhood. This helps keep the area pleasant for residents. New coffeeshops will not be located near schools.

123222.explanation: Schiedam continues to focus on affordable housing: at least 40% of the homes should be social rental housing. This keeps living affordable and gives more people access to good and suitable housing.

123229.explanation: Temporary housing, such as flexible homes, can help with the current housing shortage. They provide quick accommodations for people who find it difficult to secure housing, such as young people. Circular flexible homes are also sustainable and easy to integrate.

123230.explanation: Poor housing policies have led to housing shortages. More affordable housing and social rental homes are needed. Priority should be determined on a case-by-case basis, regardless of the applicant’s background.

123174.explanation: It would be nice if there is a balanced distribution, but that depends on the parties involved.

123177.explanation: Good agreements must be made in our hospitality policy regarding opening hours, with nuisance and similar issues acting as possible limitations.

123178.explanation: This depends on agreements made about asylum seeker reception; status holders are different. We already have a housing shortage for our own residents and prefer additional homes for target groups currently on the waiting list, such as young people and the elderly. Therefore, there must be an end date.

123180.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers must, in principle, return, so their stay should be limited as much as possible, with a defined end date.

123184.explanation: Because fireworks are now banned, this is a good alternative for the municipality to organize something similar.

123186.explanation: There are noise standards for parties and festivals that must be met; this also protects people’s health (hearing). If people experience nuisance, it must be addressed.

123187.explanation: Entrepreneurs must ensure their venues are reachable and accessible for their visitors. The municipality must ensure that all its own buildings are accessible to everyone.

123188.explanation: This is not a government obligation. If the municipality attaches great importance to the local broadcaster, a subsidy could possibly be arranged through conditions, but this would involve performance agreements.

123189.explanation: The municipality shares responsibility for school buildings. School buildings must be accessible and safe for our schoolchildren. Annual evaluations may be necessary to determine what is required.

123190.explanation: Sports clubs are important for the development and relaxation of our residents (all ages). Tailored solutions are needed, looking at what is desirable and necessary. Sport and exercise are important for the health of our Schiedam residents.

123193.explanation: Schiedam ranks high in terms of insecurity; there are several places suitable for camera surveillance to safeguard the safety of our property and people. More effort should be focused on this.

123195.explanation: Currently, BOAs (public enforcement officers) are not sufficiently protected to act effectively in situations. A bodycam and a baton could be useful tools. Their safety is also at stake; without these, they cannot perform their duties.

123200.explanation: Not all social assistance recipients are able to contribute back to society. Each recipient must be assessed individually to identify options to reduce their distance to the labor market and to prevent loneliness.

123201.explanation: People living at or below the social minimum must be helped to prevent their situation from worsening. The municipality has a care obligation to support and assist its residents when needed.

123204.explanation: This is only possible if Schiedam has sufficient finances to cover it. Encouraging people to move from benefits to work requires more than just a basic income.

123205.explanation: Each area must be assessed for what is needed, which can be done well through citizen participation. Charging stations take up parking spaces in the neighborhood. This needs to be reviewed on a neighborhood-by-neighborhood basis.

123207.explanation: Given the limitations public transport imposes on various Schiedam neighborhoods — it is almost impossible to travel from north to south by public transport — it would be very good if several trials with local transport are introduced, provided they are accessible to all residents.

123208.explanation: Our old town center cannot handle additional pollution well. It has previously been suggested that delivery of goods to shops in the center can be handled differently, for example by creating a transshipment point in the outer district. Diesel should be banned.

123209.explanation: Parking problems can possibly be solved only with citizen participation and support. The design of neighborhoods can also offer solutions to insufficient parking spaces.

123210.explanation: It is good if care providers can arrange services accommodating the diverse backgrounds of people, but that is not a guarantee. People who need care must simply get it; no restrictions should be imposed.

123211.explanation: Youth care must be efficient and of high quality. Since Schiedam needs to invest more in youth care, factors other than finances are important. A coordinator who has everything under control and delivers/directs qualitative tailored care is necessary.

123212.explanation: The municipality must ensure municipal taxes remain as low as possible. Unfortunately, these revenues are often used for various other municipal expenses and do not benefit citizens.

123214.explanation: A comparison should be made with the national average. Schiedam has never actively pursued this over the years, so it makes sense to deliberate on this to make visible how many visitors stay overnight.

123215.explanation: A number of locations in Schiedam have been designated as business parks. This also relates to environmental zoning. Expansion would come at the expense of other uses, such as residential areas, so it is not desirable.

123219.explanation: Our municipality does not have much involvement here. Solar panels are mainly placed on the roofs of buildings in Schiedam.

123220.explanation: There have been longstanding plans to improve our city entrances and to enhance access and visibility of Theater aan de Schie; this should not be delayed again. After all, it is one of our key attractions.

123221.explanation: There is a policy that includes a maximum number of coffeeshops, and that is fine. There is no added value in adding another coffeeshop.

123222.explanation: Schiedam must adhere to national standards. Many buildings have been demolished recently with (too) little social housing rebuilt. New construction is often unaffordable for many Schiedam residents, considering the average income of our citizens.

123229.explanation: Temporary housing does not solve the housing shortage because there is no flow. Hence, it is better to invest in housing for target groups such as young people, starters, and the elderly, which allows flow to occur.

123230.explanation: Waiting lists in Schiedam for social rental housing are long. Therefore, local residents should have better chances for housing compared to people from outside the city. Possibly economic ties should also be considered.

statements.title: Many more temporary homes should be provided in Schiedam.
The municipality should invest in tailored transport between the local centers (for example a dial‑a‑ride bus).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in

titleParts.glossary: A type of public transport that doesn't run on a fixed schedule but can be called to pick you up.
Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.
A bus that only runs if you reserve it in advance. The bus then takes you door to door.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Schiedam.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Business parks in Schiedam should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Schiedam should (continue to) provide shelter for rejected asylum seekers.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The BOAs who monitor safety in Schiedam should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Schiedam controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: Schiedam should introduce a parking permit in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Schiedam moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Of the newly built homes in Schiedam, at least 30% must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rentals.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Schiedam moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Schiedam must renovate Theater aan de Schie.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Schiedam must run a basic income pilot.

titleParts.text: Schiedam moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a new coffeeshop (cannabis coffee shop).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Schiedam must introduce an environmental zone to exclude polluting vehicles from the city center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Schiedam must give priority to local residents when allocating rental homes.
